title: Cephalomanes boryanum
intro: Cephalomanes boryanum, also known as the forked spleenwort or forked corydalis, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asian cultures, and is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cephalomanes boryanum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Cephalomanes boryanum is commonly made into a tea or decoction, and is also used in combination with other herbs in formulations aimed at promoting respiratory health and reducing inflammation.
taxonomy: Cephalomanes boryanum is classified within the family Hymenophyllaceae, which comprises of ferns belonging to the division Pteridophyta. The species is also classified as a member of the genus Cephalomanes, which is a part of the order Hymenophyllales. The classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics and genetic studies. At the family level, Cephalomanes boryanum is closely related to other species of the Hymenophyllaceae family. This classification is essential for understanding the evolutionary relationships among different species of ferns.
uses: Cephalomanes boryanum has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. Its medicinal uses include treating fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and reduce swelling. Additionally, it is used in traditional remedies to treat respiratory problems, such as coughs and bronchitis. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of its medicinal uses.

preparations: Cephalomanes boryanum, a species of fern, has been used in traditional herbal medicine. It is typically used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. Preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are applied externally or ingested to alleviate symptoms. The exact preparation methods and dosages may vary depending on the region and cultural practices. However, it is essential to note that the effectiveness and safety of these preparations have not been extensively scientifically studied.
